_meta.description: Static precomputed npcId -> {race, gender, region?} lookup plus cloud voice profiles, baked into the plugin. Generated offline by tools/generate_npc_voices.py from the Old School RuneScape Wiki (Infobox NPC). Do not hand-edit; edit tools/overrides.json or tools/profiles.json and regenerate.
_meta.source: oldschool.runescape.wiki Infobox NPC (race/gender/leagueRegion/location), cross-referenced by name against a full id dump for variant ids, + curated tools/overrides.json; profiles from tools/profiles.json.

profiles._comment: Authoritative, hand-curated character voice profiles for the cloud (Gemini) TTS backend. A profile is a delivery template (name, accent, style, pace) the backend renders into a Gemini AUDIO PROFILE / DIRECTOR'S NOTES block prepended to the spoken line; chat-head emotion is layered on top as a separate inline tag. This file is the SOURCE: tools/generate_npc_voices.py embeds it verbatim into src/main/resources/npc-voices.json under the top-level 'profiles' key. Edit here, then regenerate. This is a BRITISH medieval fantasy world: every character defaults to a British accent unless lore or trope says otherwise. Resolution combines every matching layer: default -> byRace[race] -> byEthnicity[ethnicity] -> every byCategory whose keyword matches the display name -> byId[npcId]. 'style' accumulates across the matching layers; 'name', 'accent', and 'pace' take the most specific layer that sets them. 'default' must be complete (all four fields); every other layer is sparse, contributing only the fields it sets. byId entries are per-NPC bespoke overrides keyed by the live NPC id (NPCComposition#getId), carrying only what is unique about that character.

default.name: Gielinor Commoner
default.accent: Common British English, the plain, earthy speech of an ordinary medieval peasant or townsperson, not posh or refined.
default.style: A grounded medieval fantasy townsperson. Plain, sincere, and natural, with the easy warmth of someone going about their day.
default.pace: Steady and conversational, with natural pauses at the punctuation.

player.name: Adventurer
player.accent: Common British English, the plain, down-to-earth speech of an ordinary adventurer, not posh or refined.
player.style: A seasoned medieval fantasy adventurer: brave and resolute, carrying the quiet confidence of someone who has seen the whole of Gielinor.
player.pace: Even and assured, unhurried but purposeful.

Human.name: Gielinor Townsfolk
Human.accent: Common British English, the plain, earthy speech of an ordinary medieval commoner, not posh or refined.
Human.style: An ordinary citizen of Gielinor. Down-to-earth, sincere, and approachable.

Elf.name: Elf of Tirannwn
Elf.accent: Posh, refined British English, an elegant Received Pronunciation with a faint melodic lilt.
Elf.style: An ancient, otherworldly elf. Graceful and soft-spoken, serene and knowing, speaking with quiet wisdom as if from beyond the mortal world.
Elf.pace: Slow and flowing, with a graceful, deliberate cadence.

Dwarf.name: Keldagrim Dwarf
Dwarf.accent: Gruff Scottish English, as heard in Glasgow.
Dwarf.style: A stout, hard-bitten mountain dwarf. Rough, gravelly, and blunt, with the no-nonsense grit of a lifetime at the forge.
Dwarf.pace: Firm and forthright, clipped and a little impatient.

Goblin.name: Goblin
Goblin.accent: Scrappy British English with an East London edge.
Goblin.style: A small, crude, mischievous goblin. High-pitched, reedy, and nasal, sniggering and excitable, never quite trustworthy.
Goblin.pace: Quick and jittery, words tumbling out in bursts.

Gnome.name: Gnome
Gnome.accent: A country Irish accent, the warm rural lilt of the Irish countryside.
Gnome.style: A small, brisk, brainy gnome. Chatty, clever, and self-important, brimming with opinions and schemes.
Gnome.pace: Fast and animated, tripping along eagerly.

Troll.name: Troll
Troll.accent: British English, South London Brixton accent.
Troll.style: A huge, slow, simple-minded troll, good-natured but dim. A very deep, low, booming, rumbling voice, lumbering and pleased with itself, easily confused.
Troll.pace: Slow and heavy, with long thinking pauses.

Undead.name: The Undead
Undead.accent: British English, Received Pronunciation, cold and hollow.
Undead.style: A restless undead thing dragged back from the grave. Hollow, breathy, and sepulchral, the voice echoing as if from a long way off.
Undead.pace: Slow and dragging, with an eerie, drawn-out delivery.

Demon.name: Demon
Demon.accent: Deep, resonant British English with a menacing edge.
Demon.style: A vast, malevolent demon from the abyss. Booming, guttural, and contemptuous, radiating ancient threat.
Demon.pace: Slow and deliberate, each word landing like a hammer.

Wizard.name: Wizard
Wizard.accent: Distinguished elderly British English, Received Pronunciation.
Wizard.style: A wise and learned old wizard, a sage and storyteller. Warm, knowing, and unhurried, weighty with deep arcane knowledge.
Wizard.pace: Measured and thoughtful, with deliberate, knowing pauses.

byCategory.name: Leprechaun
byCategory.accent: Irish English, as heard in Dublin.
byCategory.style: A jaunty, twinkle-eyed leprechaun. Playful, lilting, and full of cheek, forever amused at its own jokes.
byCategory.pace: Quick and lively, with a bouncing, sing-song rhythm.

byCategory.name: Vampyre
byCategory.accent: Aristocratic Eastern European, a Transylvanian accent in the manner of Dracula.
byCategory.style: An ancient, predatory vampyre noble. Silken, cold, and menacing, dripping with disdain for lesser mortals.
byCategory.pace: Slow and hypnotic, savouring every word with quiet menace.

byCategory.name: Fremennik
byCategory.accent: A fantasy Norse accent, a Scandinavian-flavoured English in the manner of a Viking warrior from the old sagas.
byCategory.style: A hardy Fremennik Viking of the frozen north. Proud, hearty, and weathered, bred to longship, axe, and saga.
byCategory.pace: Strong and steady, robust and deliberate.

byCategory.name: TzHaar
byCategory.accent: Deep, rumbling British English, slow and elemental.
byCategory.style: A living creature of molten rock from the city of TzHaar. Vast, stony, and rumbling, calm but immense, speaking of honour and combat.
byCategory.pace: Very slow and ponderous, like grinding stone.

byCategory.name: Dragon
byCategory.accent: Vast, resonant British English, ancient and imperious.
byCategory.style: An ancient, colossal dragon. Booming, regal, and contemptuous of mortals, every word freighted with age and power.
byCategory.pace: Slow and grand, rolling and unhurried.

byCategory.name: Imp
byCategory.accent: Squeaky, high-pitched British English, fast and impish.
byCategory.style: A tiny, hyperactive imp. Shrill, gleeful, and mischievous, cackling and impossible to pin down.
byCategory.pace: Extremely fast and erratic, words spilling over each other.

byCategory.name: Fairy
byCategory.accent: Light, airy British English, bright and delicate.
byCategory.style: A small, sparkling fairy. Sweet, breezy, and quick, flitting from thought to thought with childlike delight.
byCategory.pace: Quick and light, with a fluttering, buoyant lilt.

byCategory.name: Ogre
byCategory.accent: Big, dim British English with a West Midlands burr.
byCategory.style: A huge, slow-witted ogre with a very deep, low, booming voice. Loud, blunt, and lumbering, friendly enough but not the sharpest.
byCategory.pace: Slow and heavy, with plodding, simple phrasing.

byCategory.name: Ghost
byCategory.accent: Distant, breathy British English, Received Pronunciation, faint and mournful.
byCategory.style: A lingering, sorrowful spirit. Wispy, far-off, and forlorn, half-faded from the world of the living.
byCategory.pace: Slow and drifting, trailing off into silence.

byCategory.name: Pirate
byCategory.accent: Rough West Country British English, a Cornish seafarer's drawl.
byCategory.style: A salty, swaggering pirate. Gravelly, hearty, and roguish, all bluster and grog and tall tales.
byCategory.pace: Loose and rollicking, with a swaggering swing.

byCategory.name: Barbarian
byCategory.accent: Broad, hearty Northern English, as heard in Yorkshire.
byCategory.style: A boisterous barbarian of the wilds. Loud, blunt, and full-blooded, proud of strength and scornful of soft folk.
byCategory.pace: Forceful and robust, full of gusto.

byCategory.name: Wizard
byCategory.style: A wise and learned spellcaster, a sage and keeper of arcane lore; knowing and unhurried, speaking in a measured, scholarly register weighty with deep magical knowledge.
byCategory.pace: Measured and thoughtful, with deliberate, knowing pauses.

byCategory.name: Devotee
byCategory.style: A pious monastic of the faith; calm, kindly, and serene, speaking in a gentle, devout register with quiet, unshakeable conviction.

byCategory.name: Royalty
byCategory.style: A sovereign of the realm; regal, authoritative, and gracious, speaking in a refined, aristocratic, elevated register, every word carrying the weight of a crown.

byCategory.name: Knight
byCategory.style: A noble knight of the realm; honourable, disciplined, and gallant, speaking in a crisp, formal, well-bred register weighted with chivalric duty.
byCategory.pace: Firm and measured, crisp and commanding.

byCategory.name: Noble
byCategory.style: A highborn aristocrat of the realm; polished, urbane, and self-assured, speaking in a refined, well-bred register, accustomed to deference.

byEthnicity._comment: Accent layer keyed by an NPC's ethnicity (where they are from, baked per-NPC into npcs[id].ethnicity; defaulted from the wiki leagueRegion and corrected in overrides.json for foreigners). Applied only to the plain folk (Human / unknown race) so it tints them with local colour, while distinctive races (dwarf, elf, troll, goblin, undead, demon, wizard) keep their racial accent wherever they are. A keyword category (vampyre, ghost) or a bespoke byId entry can still override. Sparse and accent-only, so it does not dilute the persona blend. The desert and far lands follow the real-world cultures they are based on; the central British kingdoms use distinct English regional accents for variety. NPCs with no single ethnicity keep the common British default.

misthalin.accent: Common British English, the plain, standard speech of Gielinor's central kingdom of Misthalin.

asgarnia.accent: A West Country British English, the rural burr of the Asgarnian countryside.

kandarin.accent: A Northern English accent, as heard in Yorkshire, for the folk of Kandarin.

kourend.accent: A broad, rustic British English, the working speech of the Kingdom of Great Kourend.

wilderness.accent: A rough, harsh British English, the hard edge of the outlaws of the Wilderness.

tirannwn.accent: A Welsh accent, lilting and lyrical, of the western land of Tirannwn.

varlamore.accent: A strong, clearly Italian accent, the lyrical melodic cadence of a native Italian speaking English, of the sun-baked realm of Varlamore.

kharidian.accent: A Middle Eastern accent, Arabic-inflected English, as spoken in the desert cities of the Kharidian.

menaphite.accent: An Egyptian accent, the cadence of ancient Menaphos and Sophanem.

karamja.accent: A West African accent, Nigerian-inflected English, as spoken on the jungle isle of Karamja.

morytania.accent: An Eastern European accent, the cold, gothic lilt of the cursed land of Morytania.

fremennik.accent: A fantasy Norse accent, a Scandinavian-flavoured English in the manner of a Viking from the old sagas.

easternlands.accent: A Japanese accent, refined and precise, of the distant Eastern Lands across the sea.

byId._comment: Per-NPC bespoke overrides keyed by live NPC id (NPCComposition#getId). Sparse: only fields unique to the character. Inherits accent/pace (and name, if omitted) from the matched category/region/race/default layers.

3105.name: Hans
3105.style: An eccentric, doddery old groundskeeper who has shuffled around Lumbridge Castle for decades; rambling, friendly, oddly proud of his longevity.
3105.pace: Slow and shuffling, wandering between thoughts.

306.name: Lumbridge Guide
306.style: A patient, welcoming older man who orients newcomers; calm, helpful, and mildly officious.

4626.name: Cook
4626.style: A flustered, fretful castle cook in a perpetual flap over his kitchen; scatterbrained and self-important.

2812.name: Father Aereck
2812.style: A gentle, earnest, soft-spoken parish priest; mild-mannered and a touch timid.

10619.name: Bob
10619.style: A laid-back, chatty axe-shop owner; easygoing and casual, a bit of a rambler who loves to gab.

1305.name: Hairdresser
1305.style: A bubbly, talkative Falador stylist; upbeat, breezy, and a little vain about appearances.

732.name: Fred the Farmer
732.style: A gruff, plain-spoken working farmer; blunt, no-nonsense, and rustic.

4628.name: Gillie Groats
4628.style: A cheery, down-to-earth milkmaid; simple, sweet, and chatty.

2147.name: Veos
2147.style: An earnest, purposeful seafarer with a calm, faintly devout streak; steady and measured.

3308.name: Gielinor Guide
3308.style: A warm, encouraging instructor for brand-new adventurers; clear, patient, and didactic.

4243.name: Reldo
4243.style: A bookish, mild-mannered royal librarian; precise and slightly pedantic, quietly enthusiastic about history and lore.

2886.name: Aubury
2886.style: An eccentric, slightly distracted rune-shop wizard; affable but absent-minded, with an offhand scholarly oddness.

5005.name: Wizard Mizgog
5005.style: A cranky, irritable old wizard; gruff, impatient, and blunt, forever grumbling about younger folk.

11868.name: Gypsy Aris
11868.style: A theatrical, knowing fortune-teller; mysterious and dramatic, speaking in cryptic, leading prophecy.

5037.name: Romeo
5037.style: A naive, lovestruck young romantic; gushing, dim-witted, and overdramatic, with his head in the clouds.

5035.name: Juliet
5035.style: A gentle, earnest young noblewoman; soft-spoken, sincere, and romantic, a touch melodramatic.

3480.name: Dr Harlow
3480.style: A washed-up, perpetually sozzled vampire-hunter; gruff and rambling, far more interested in his next drink than the conversation.

4771.name: Sir Amik Varze
4771.style: The stately commander of the White Knights; authoritative, formal, and dignified, measured and noble in tone.

4687.name: Sir Tiffy Cashien
4687.style: A genteel, chipper old Temple Knight recruiter; warmly polite and grandfatherly, all mild upper-class charm and good humour.

2108.name: Wise Old Man
2108.style: A venerable, world-weary retired adventurer; calm, sage, and dryly self-assured, speaking with patient gravitas.

815.name: Duke Horacio
815.style: A portly, genial nobleman of middling competence; pompous but kindly, and fond of his own title.

5215.name: King Roald
5215.style: A formal, regal monarch; measured and slightly aloof, speaking with stately, bureaucratic authority.

923.name: Father Urhney
923.style: A reclusive hermit priest; grumpy, irritable, and curt, resentful of being disturbed in his swamp.

3500.name: Gertrude
3500.style: A busy, fussy working mother; brisk and talkative, brusque but ultimately warm-hearted.

4619.name: Hetty
4619.style: A sly old village witch; sing-song, mysterious, and faintly mocking, relishing the dark and arcane.

5081.name: Wizard Traiborn
5081.style: A doddering, absent-minded old wizard; eccentric, rambling, and forgetful, forever wandering off-topic.

3893.name: Doric
3893.style: A hardy, plain-spoken dwarf smith; gruff but good-natured and welcoming to those who lend a hand.
3893.accent: Gruff Scottish English, as heard in Glasgow.

822.name: Oziach
822.style: A blunt, no-nonsense armour seller; gruff and dismissive, unimpressed and curt with strangers.

9636.name: Drezel
9636.style: A meek, devout monk; gentle, humble, and earnest, soft-spoken and grateful for any aid.

4280.name: Ned
4280.style: An old salt-of-the-earth Draynor villager and rope-maker; folksy, rough-edged, and chatty.

2989.name: Ak-Haranu
2989.style: A worldly, shrewd traveller from the distant Eastern Lands; courteous and composed, with a gambler's quiet, cunning confidence.
